zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth De Leybourne於1310年出生在英國諾圖波藍德韋斯特 利爾本時，她的父親Sir John Lilburne31歲，母親Constance Pontop47歲。她和Robert Bowes至少有3個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1384年在英國達勒姆享年74歲。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Leybourne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Laybourne
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from Leyburn in Yorkshire. The placename probably derives from Old English hlēg ‘shelter’ + burna ‘spring, stream’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth De Leybourne was born in 1310, in West Lilburn, Northumberland, England, her father, Sir John Lilburne, was 31 and her mother, Constance Pontop, was 47. She had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Robert Bowes. She died in 1384, in Durham, England, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth De Leybourne est née en 1310, à West Lilburn, Northumberland, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Lilburne, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Constance Pontop, avait 47 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Robert Bowes. Elle est décédée en 1384, à Durham, Angleterre, à l’âge de 74 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth De Leybourne(이)가 1310년 영국 노르스벌랜드 웨스트 릴번에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Lilburne(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Constance Pontop(은)는 47세였다. 그녀는 Robert Bowes(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1384년 영국 뒤람에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth De Leybourne родилась в 1310 г. в местности вест лилбурн, нортумбирланд, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Lilburne было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Constance Pontop, 47. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери от Robert Bowes. Она умерла в 1384 г. в местности дурам, Англия в возрасте 74 лет.
